Aerospace Apr 2, 2026

Swift spacecraft reorientation buys time for reboost mission

NASA modified operations of an astrophysics spacecraft in a decaying orbit to buy more time for a mission later this year that will attempt to raise its orbit.

NASA's operational flexibility in spacecraft attitude control demonstrates how ground teams can extend mission life through strategic maneuvering while planning infrastructure upgrades.

See SpaceNews →

Investigators: LaGuardia Controller May Have Stepped Away Before Deadly Crash

The incident quickly turned tragic.

Operational fatigue and inadequate staffing protocols may have created vulnerability gaps in tower coverage during critical traffic handling periods.

The Real Reason Why Rolls-Royce Owns The Airbus A350 Engine Slot

A closer look at the host of advancements offered by the Trent XWB, and how it continues to develop.

Rolls-Royce's dominance in A350 powerplants stems from the Trent XWB's architectural advantages in fuel efficiency and modular upgrade capability, securing long-term aftermarket revenue.

Air Cargo Apr 2, 2026

5 Reasons Why Airlines Still Fly The Boeing 747 Freighter

The Boeing 747 freighter remains valuable for its payload, cargo volume, nose-loading capability, and long-haul performance.

The 747F's nose-loading door and 140-ton payload capacity make it irreplaceable for oversize cargo that modern narrowbody freighters cannot accommodate, sustaining its viability despite older design.

Business Aviation Apr 2, 2026

The Sky’s Greatest Dragon: Drake’s Luxurious 30-Year Old Boeing 767 Returns To The Skies

Despite its age, the Boeing 767 platform continues to offer strong structural reliability and adaptability for VIP transport roles.

The 767's proven 30-year longevity demonstrates that premium cabin retrofits can extend high-value aircraft viability well beyond typical replacement cycles, reducing capital expenditure for ultra-wealthy operators.

US sends EA-37B Compass Call into Iran fight in first known combat deployment

The US military has introduced a potent new weapon into the Iran fight with the deployment of the…...

The EA-37B Compass Call's Iranian deployment marks the first operational use of this advanced electronic warfare platform in active combat, significantly enhancing US air dominance capabilities in contested airspace.

Regulatory Apr 2, 2026

FAA Releases Part 141 Modernization Proposal

Report outlines proposals to alter certificated flight school approval, oversight and training technology.

The modernization proposal signals the FAA's recognition that flight school regulations haven't kept pace with contemporary training technologies, potentially accelerating adoption of sim-based curricula and competency-based progression across the industry.

A closer look at the most popular hub in the Great White North.

Toronto Pearson's dominance as Canada's busiest airport reflects concentration of international connectivity and revenue in a single hub, creating both competitive advantages and infrastructure vulnerability for the domestic network.

General Aviation Apr 2, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

Eastern Iowa Airport's diverse traffic mix from training aircraft to air carriers positions it as a critical regional hub for both pilot development and commercial connectivity.

Pentagon awards Raytheon $45 million for GPS ground system as program future is reassessed

The ‘unpriced change order’ supports satellite launches while officials reassess long-delayed ground system...

Raytheon's funding extension buys time for Pentagon officials to fundamentally rethink GPS modernization strategy amid repeated delays and cost overruns.

Commercial Apr 2, 2026

Here’s How Much Wider The Airbus A350 Cabin Is Compared To The Boeing 787 Dreamliner

The Airbus A350 cabin is 5 inches wider than the Boeing 787—here’s how that small difference transforms seat comfort and long-haul travel.

The A350's 5-inch cabin width advantage enables airlines to configure 10-abreast seating versus the 787's 9-abreast, creating significant revenue and passenger experience differentiation on long-haul routes.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Apr 2, 2026

Artemis II Astronauts Launch to Moon

NASA’s Space Launch System rocket and Orion spacecraft lift off in this April 1, 2026, image. NASA’s Artemis II mission will take NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch, and CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy on an approximately 10-day journey around the Moon and...

Artemis II's planned 2026 launch represents a critical validation of SLS/Orion systems before crewed lunar landing attempts, with implications for aerospace supply chain and mission-critical component reliability.

See NASA →

Industry Apr 2, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across all aviation segments can simultaneously address capacity constraints and environmental impacts, with significant research gaps remaining in emerging sustainability technologies.

See MDPI →

Regulatory Apr 2, 2026

Washington Repeals Aircraft Tax Before It Takes Effect

Measure enacted in 2025 is replaced with fuel tax and fee increases.

Washington's shift from aircraft sales tax to fuel-based taxation redistributes costs from purchasers to operators, potentially affecting fleet purchasing patterns and operating economics across the state.

See AVweb →

Military Apr 2, 2026

Global Combat Air Programme Awards First Contract with Edgewing

The first joint international contract has been awarded for the Global Combat Air Programme (GCAP), focusing on key design and engineering activities. The GCAP International Government Organisation (GIGO), which represents the governments of the UK, Italy and Japan, awarded a £686 million (about...

The £686 million initial contract signals the GCAP partnership is moving from concept to concrete engineering phases, establishing integrated development infrastructure across three nations.

Software-defined avionics architectures are replacing legacy hardware-centric systems, fundamentally shifting MRO economics and crew training requirements across the industry.

Climate-driven turbulence patterns are exceeding historical design parameters, requiring aviation to fundamentally reassess aircraft certification and cockpit detection systems.

Military Apr 2, 2026

Army’s SkyFoundry Plan To Build Own Cheap Drones Has ‘Evolved,’ Official Notes Commercial Involvement

The Army’s SkyFoundry initiative to manufacture thousands of its own cheap drones on a monthly basis has recently “evolved,” with an official telling sister publication Defense Daily the program is “100 […]...

The Army's shift toward commercial partnerships for SkyFoundry signals a potential pivot from independent drone manufacturing to leveraging existing industry capacity and expertise.

Autonomous flight integration testing with third-party software demonstrates industry movement toward certified autonomous operations beyond current regulations.

Military Apr 2, 2026

Army Accepts First Optionally-Piloted Black Hawk, Will Inform Scaling Autonomy Across The Fleet

The Army announced Friday it has officially accepted the first Black Hawk helicopter outfitted to fly autonomously, with the plans for the aircraft to now undergo a “rigorous testing phase.” […]...

Successful autonomous Black Hawk integration establishes the validation pathway for retrofitting thousands of existing aircraft, potentially reducing pilot workload and enabling operations in high-risk environments without redesigning platforms.
